user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Azure Ridge Partners.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 10:47 EST
Subject: Follow-up: deployment blockers Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 16:42 EST
Subject: Re: deployment blockers Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 12:46 EST
Subject: deployment blockers - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with deployment blockers. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-02-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:06:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:08:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:12:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:19:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:25:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:31:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:34:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Azure Ridge Partners: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
